heading: Choosing a Release Cadence
body:
Starting with MongoDB 8.2, two release cadence options are available for
Atlas Dedicated clusters:

- Major Versions: You can choose to stay on a specific major version
  such as MongoDB 8.0. Clusters on major versions receive bug fixes
  and security patches. You must manually upgrade clusters before
  their End of Life (EOL). Otherwise, Atlas automatically upgrades
  clusters to the latest supported major version after EOL.

- Latest Version with Auto-Upgrade: If you set your cluster to this
  release option, it receives automatic upgrades to the latest available
  MongoDB major or minor version and enables new features.

  To learn more about manually upgrading your Atlas cluster,
  see [upgrade your cluster to MongoDB 8.0](upgrade-major-MongoDB-version).

> **Note:** You cannot choose a release option for clusters on the Free and Flex
> tiers as these clusters are automatically upgraded.

You can revert a cluster that follows the minor version cadence to
the major version cadence by selecting the most recent major version
from the **Select a Version** dropdown menu. However, you can
only do this before the first minor version release of the major version
is available. After a cluster updates from a major version to a minor
version, you cannot revert the cluster until the next major version
release.

To learn more about MongoDB versions, see [MongoDB Versioning](manual/reference/versioning).
